title: The Wind

evidence.note: Published 1928; the 95-year US copyright term has expired for works published through 1930, so the film print is public domain in the US by term expiry.

watch.label: Internet Archive — The Wind (1928), full film

editorial: The Wind is a 1928 American silent drama directed by Victor Sjostrom for Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer, starring Lillian Gish, Lars Hanson and Montagu Love, adapted by Frances Marion from Dorothy Scarborough's novel. One of the last major silent films MGM released, it is widely regarded as a masterpiece of the silent form and one of Gish's finest performances. Published in 1928, the film print's 95-year US copyright term has expired, placing the photoplay in the public domain by term expiry.
